title: spartina maritima subsp. stricta
intro: Spartina maritima subsp. stricta, also known as little salt grass or salt hay, is a subspecies of salt-tolerant grass that grows in coastal areas. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with its roots and leaves having anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Spartina maritima subsp. stricta include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support immune system health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its medicinal properties can also be extracted and used in topical applications.
